76242.subject: рџ”· The Daily: Strategy's bitcoin acquisition drought continues
76242.body:
Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin 'vibe-codes' anonymous billboard demo with onchain moderation on Aztec | Hyperliquid's HIP-4 to support permissionless deployment for outcome markets | Bernstein lifts Robinhood target to $160, sees prediction markets revenue overtaking crypto | Saylor urges Bitcoin to reject BIP-110 in 110-point essay as soft fork's August showdown approaches |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Jason Shubnell

Happy Monday! рџЏ† *Spain* lifted the *World Cup* on Sunday, capping a tournament that also sent prediction market activity into extra time, with combined *Kalshi* and *Polymarket* trading volume surging to record levels ( https://www.theblock.co/data/decentralized-finance/prediction-markets?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) in June and July.

In today's newsletter, *Strategy's bitcoin* acquisition drought continues, *Vitalik Buterin* "vibe-codes" on *Aztec* , *Hyperliquid* plans a permissionless deployment for outcome markets, and more.

Meanwhile, former *New York* Governor *Andrew Cuomo* joined ( https://www.theblock.co/post/408938/former-ny-governor-cuomo-joins-okx-board-touts-24-7-tokenized-trading?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) *OKX's* board of directors amid hopes to expand its U.S. footprint.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read вЂ” and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*рџ’ё Strategy sells $263.5 million in MSTR shares, buys no bitcoin as USD reserve tops $3.2 billion*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/408892/strategy-sells-263-5-million-in-mstr-shares-buys-no-bitcoin-as-usd-reserve-tops-3-2-billion?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

*Strategy* sold about $263.5 million worth of *MSTR* shares last week but neither bought nor sold any *bitcoin* , according to an 8-K filing with the *Securities and Exchange Commission* on Monday.

* The firm used the proceeds to lift its *USD reserve* by $225 million to $3.225 billion as of July 19, and made no repurchases under its share buyback programs.
* Strategy held its bitcoin treasury at 843,775 BTC вЂ” around 4% of supply and worth $54.7 billion but carrying roughly $9 billion in paper losses вЂ” bought at an average price of $75,476.
* The company's strategy has varied from its regular bitcoin acquisition cadence in recent weeks, predominantly choosing to build up its USD reserve instead, alongside some BTC sales.
*

76238.subject: Data & Insights: Strategy's Collapse, RWA's Rise
76238.body:
The Data and Insights newsletter brings the highlights of the week from The BlockвЂ™s Data Dashboard

( https://www.theblock.co/data/crypto-markets/spot )

Gm data enthusiasts! What a way to wrap up the World Cup. Now that we're no longer glued to our screens, our team recently set up a new newsletter titled " Tokenization Briefing ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters )." With trillions of dollars of real-world assets migrating onto blockchain rails we are introducing a way for readers to keep up to date with market insights and visual analyses on what matters in the world of Tokenization. Sign up *here* ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters ).

This week, weвЂ™re taking a look at altcoin ETFs and Strategy mNAV. WeвЂ™ll also look at tokenized assets, Robinhood, and prediction market data. So grab a drink, take a seat, and let's dive in.

1: Altcoin Alternatives

( https://www.theblock.co/data/etfs/etf-comparison/spot-etf-market-share-by-coin-excluding-btc-and-eth )

Chart via The Block's Data Dashboard

*Key Insights* provided by The Block Research

* While weвЂ™ve been focusing on Bitcoin and Ethereum spot ETFs, altcoin ETFs have continued to trade under most people's radar. Solana and Hyperliquid ETFs have combined to account for nearly 80% of non-BTC and ETH ETF volume, with Hyperliquid ETFs having launched only 2 months ago.

* Solana boasts an impressive $904M AUM, while the Hyperliquid suite has managed to attract $350M in net inflows. Both figures represent approximately 2% of each token's market cap.
* Bitcoin, by comparison, has nearly 9% of its market cap held in ETF products. The gap suggests that altcoin ETFs may benefit further as they gain investor acceptance.
* Alternatively, this could be reflected in BTCвЂ™s nearly 2-year head start rather than a ceiling that alts are guaranteed to close.

* These flows are likely skewed by investor type. SOL and HYPE sit further down the risk curve with less regulatory precedent and higher volatility, so they're attracting allocators with higher risk tolerance. Bitcoin and Ethereum, however, benefit from the anchoring of investors who tend to be stickier and more passive.

* As projects such as Hyperliquid and Solana continue to engage with regulatory bodies and build out RWA rails, this may open the door to investors interested in that exposure.

See the Interactive Chart ( https://www.theblock.co/data/etfs/etf-comparison/spot-etf-market-share-by-coin-excluding-btc-and-eth )

2: Strategic Conclusions

( https://www.theblock.co/data/treasuries/bitcoin-treasuries/strategy-mstr-premium-discount-to-nav )

Chart via The Block's Data Dashboard

*Key Insights* provided by The Block Research

* MSTR is down roughly 38% this year, with this market cap now sitting at just under $35B. This comes as its Bitcoin stack is worth $54.5B, or 0.63x when you divide market cap by the value of its BTC.

* This is a simple mNAV and fails to capture the leverage that is included on top of

76237.subject: Boomers have the good life, but it could be longer
76237.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDN9AAcAAAAABDudPcrwR9Y368m2yREITlu8P6wDvmk4xxsztl_t50krAyHDqVfVw0SSZabYJoFtu_SRc-a_u0XEhUAuJSU7ou15sZyfEwkjUHSu3ay__KVT8Q 

July 20th 2026

 

 
 
 
The Economist Today

 Our best journalism, handpicked each day


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDN9AAcAAAAABDudPcrxBQqtdXnzkPfFbvSvANghIY1O2NAhZKVE3E_P1LYQitXy-VgGu7CMVSOA_WubN7iK-TGphtoeLhkIFdj4gzRhARpPGpxtOhBNJzjcPQ 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDN9AAcAAAAABDudPcrydEpJ85MxrLxqvOo3DqiUdVJJyuPBhPzFZbxNQlFYcjwOv9OnEhm5JpCH6EU9wwxi74HwaMZlOO0SvP2nHPUvD5s_VOctX93oYjD_Ow 


 


 
 

Stephanie Studer

Senior digital editor



 Something is wrong with American life expectancy. Lifespans had been on a joyous forward march for over a century. After 2010 they stalled. And one generation has lost out more than any other: the boomers. A single chart traces the arc of their lives (and all of ours), so our visual team 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDN9AAcAAAAABDudPcrz6TwLzREdAQRwZ-TF1t3OczQKj8iQ_CXeJkpd0K90AlVXt3UfE9EXZzmeX8tpS1kDdXNGC_QklKz4NPrmuRzQ9ykxZaWoomsb 
tells you this story ,
 start to finish, in one gorgeous graphic.
